citation_and_locator: Lei, Margaret Ka-yan, and Thomas Hun-Tak Lee. 2017. Early Knowledge of the Interaction between Aspect and Quantification: Evidence from Child Cantonese. BUCLD 41 Proceedings:424-436; section 3.2, examples 12-16, and experiments
what_it_supports: Analyzes `saai3` as universal quantification; direct subject/object scope examples; definiteness/exhaustivity requirement; contrasts with partial-compatible `zo2`; experimental child/adult distinction.
limit: Acquisition experiments focus on selected plural-classifier and incremental-theme profiles and do not establish every verb, nominal, scope ambiguity, negation, or objectless use.

citation_and_locator: Lei, Margaret Ka-yan, and Thomas Hun-Tak Lee. 2019. Differentiating universal quantification from completive aspect in child Cantonese. In Three Streams of Generative Language Acquisition Research, pp. 159-183. DOI 10.1075/lald.63.09lei
what_it_supports: Explicitly distinguishes `saai3` universal quantifier suffix from `jyun4` completive aspect and reports systematic child distinction in completion/non-completion tasks.
limit: Abstract-level access in this pass does not supply the full stimulus inventory or every adult judgment.
